success_surface: Cell-level formula manipulation with edge-case rounding logic
effective_behaviors: Using openpyxl for formula-based cell operations to preserve Excel formula functionality
Writing formulas as raw strings with leading '=' sign for proper Excel interpretation
Targeting only specified answer cells without unnecessary modifications to other regions
Validating data structure and row alignment before formula application
Designing custom formulas to handle specific edge cases (midpoint rounding rules)
Verifying formula assignments programmatically before saving the workbook
Creating output directories automatically to ensure save operation succeeds
Saving to new file path instead of modifying source file in-place
evidence: Used wb['Sheet2'] after checking wb.sheetnames list for correct worksheet access
Applied f'=IF(MOD(B1,5)=2.5,FLOOR(B1,5),MROUND(B1,5))' as raw string to ws['C1'] and ws['C2']
Printed formula values post-assignment: 'Formulas applied: C1: ... C2: ...' for verification
Created output directory with os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_path), exist_ok=True) before wb.save()
Saved to '/mnt/data/outputs/...' path rather than modifying original '_init.xlsx' file
Only modified cells C1 and C2 as specified by answer_position constraint

memory_items.title: Formula Preservation Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When cell manipulation requires Excel formulas, use openpyxl and write formulas as raw strings prefixed with '='
memory_items.generalization: Always prefix formula content with '=' character; never evaluate formulas before writing them to cells to maintain Excel's calculation capability

memory_items.title: Edge Case Formula Design
memory_items.lesson: Complex rounding/logic requirements need custom formulas using IF conditions and appropriate functions (FLOOR, CEILING, MROUND, MOD)
memory_items.generalization: For non-standard calculations, build nested conditional formulas that explicitly handle edge cases specified in the instruction

memory_items.title: Cell-Level Scope Control
memory_items.lesson: Cell-Level Manipulation tasks require updating only the specified answer_position range
memory_items.generalization: Parse the answer_position field and limit all read/write operations strictly within those bounds

memory_items.title: Formula Verification Step
memory_items.lesson: After assigning formulas to cells, verify the value attribute contains the expected formula string before saving
memory_items.generalization: Add debug logging to confirm formula cells contain formulas (not computed values) prior to final save operation

memory_items.title: Output Directory Safety
memory_items.lesson: Before saving the workbook, ensure the output directory exists to prevent save failures
memory_items.generalization: Use os.makedirs with exist_ok=True for the output directory path before calling wb.save()

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Formula Integrity**: When manipulating cells with formulas, always write them as raw strings starting with '='. Verify formula cells contain formulas (not computed values) before saving.
- **Edge Case Logic**: For specialized calculations (e.g., midpoint rounding), construct nested conditional formulas that explicitly handle boundary conditions.
- **Scope Discipline**: For Cell-Level Manipulation tasks, restrict all modifications to the exact answer_position range specified.
- **Save Precautions**: Create the output directory before saving to prevent path-related errors.
- **Verification Logging**: After formula assignment, log the cell values to confirm formulas are properly stored.
